While the merge package needs its own Issue struct for JSONL flexibility, it could import and use types.Status for constants to get compile-time type checking.  {"id":"bd-cnwx","title":"Refactor mol.go: split 1200+ line file into subcommands","description":"## Problem

cmd/bd/mol.go has grown to 1200+ lines with all molecule subcommands in one file.

## Current State
- mol.go: 1218 lines (bond, spawn, run, distill, catalog, show, etc.)
- Hard to navigate, review, and maintain

## Proposed Structure
Split into separate files by subcommand:
```
cmd/bd/
├── mol.go           # Root command, shared helpers
├── mol_bond.go      # bd mol bond
├── mol_spawn.go     # bd mol spawn  
├── mol_run.go       # bd mol run
├── mol_distill.go   # bd mol distill
├── mol_catalog.go   # bd mol catalog
├── mol_show.go      # bd mol show
└── mol_test.go      # Tests (already separate)
```

## Benefits
- Easier code review
- Better separation of concerns
- Simpler navigation
- Each subcommand self-contained","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2025-12-21T11:30:58.832192-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T11:42:49.390824-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T11:42:49.390824-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-ldb0","title":"Rename ephemeral → wisp throughout codebase","description":"## The Change

Rename 'ephemeral' to 'wisp' throughout the beads codebase.

## Why

**Ephemeral** is:
- 4 syllables (too long)
- Greek/academic (doesn't match bond/burn/squash)
- Overused in tech (K8s, networking, storage)
- Passive/descriptive

**Wisp** is:
- 1 syllable (matches bond/burn/squash)
- Evocative - you can SEE a wisp
- Steam engine metaphor - Gas Town is engines, steam wisps rise and dissipate
- Will-o'-the-wisp - transient spirits that guide then vanish
- Unique - nobody else uses it

## The Steam Engine Metaphor

```
Engine does work → generates steam
Steam wisps rise → execution trace
Steam condenses → digest (distillate)
Steam dissipates → cleaned up (burned)
```

## Full Vocabulary

| Term | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| bond | Attach proto to work (creates wisps) |
| wisp | Temporary execution step |
| squash | Condense wisps into digest |
| burn | Destroy wisps without record |
| digest | Permanent condensed record |

## Changes Required

### Code
- `Ephemeral bool` → `Wisp bool` in types/issue.go
- `--ephemeral` flag → remove (wisp is default)
- `--persistent` flag → keep as opt-out
- `bd cleanup --ephemeral` → `bd cleanup --wisps`
- Update all references in mol_*.go files

### Docs
- Update all documentation
- Update CLAUDE.md examples
- Update CLI help text

### Database Migration
- Add migration to rename field (or keep internal name, just change API)

## Example Usage After

```bash
bd mol bond mol-polecat-work   # Creates wisps (default)
bd mol bond mol-xxx --persistent  # Creates permanent issues
bd mol squash bd-xxx           # Condenses wisps → digest
bd cleanup --wisps             # Clean old wisps
bd list --wisps                # Show wisp issues
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:44:41.576068-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T00:32:31.153738-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T00:32:31.153738-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-lfak","title":"bd preflight: PR readiness checks for contributors","description":"## Vision

Encode project-specific institutional knowledge into executable checks. CONTRIBUTING.md is documentation that's read once and forgotten; `bd preflight` is documentation that runs at exactly the right moment.

## Problem Statement

Contributors face a "last mile" problem - they do the work but stumble on project-specific gotchas at PR time:
- Nix vendorHash gets stale when go.sum changes
- Beads artifacts leak into PRs (see bd-umbf for namespace solution)
- Version mismatches between version.go and default.nix
- Tests/lint not run locally before pushing
- Other project-specific checks that only surface when CI fails

These are too obscure to remember, exist in docs nobody reads end-to-end, and waste CI round-trips.

## Why beads?

Beads already has a foothold in the contributor workflow. It knows:
- Git state (staged files, branch, dirty status)
- Project structure
- The specific issue being worked on
- Project-specific configuration

## Proposed Interface

### Tier 1: Checklist Mode (v1)

    $ bd preflight
    PR Readiness Checklist:

    [ ] Tests pass: go test -short ./...
    [ ] Lint passes: golangci-lint run ./...
    [ ] No beads pollution: check .beads/issues.jsonl diff
    [ ] Nix hash current: go.sum unchanged or vendorHash updated
    [ ] Version sync: version.go matches default.nix

    Run 'bd preflight --check' to validate automatically.

### Tier 2: Check Mode (v2)

    $ bd preflight --check
    ✓ Tests pass
    ✓ Lint passes
    ⚠ Beads pollution: 3 issues in diff - are these project issues or personal?
    ✗ Nix hash stale: go.sum changed, vendorHash needs update
      Fix: sha256-KRR6dXzsSw8OmEHGBEVDBOoIgfoZ2p0541T9ayjGHlI=
    ✓ Version sync

    1 error, 1 warning. Run 'bd preflight --fix' to auto-fix where possible.

### Tier 3: Fix Mode (v3)

    $ bd preflight --fix
    ✓ Updated vendorHash in default.nix
    ⚠ Cannot auto-fix beads pollution - manual review needed

## Checks to Implement

| Check | Description | Auto-fixable |
|-------|-------------|--------------|
| tests | Run go test -short ./... | No |
| lint | Run golangci-lint | Partial (gofmt) |
| beads-pollution | Detect personal issues in diff | No (see bd-umbf) |
| nix-hash | Detect stale vendorHash | Yes (if nix available) |
| version-sync | version.go matches default.nix | Yes |
| no-debug | No TODO/FIXME/console.log | Warn only |
| clean-stage | No unintended files staged | Warn only |

## Future: Configuration

Make checks configurable per-project via .beads/preflight.yaml:

    preflight:
      checks:
        - name: tests
          run: go test -short ./...
          required: true
        - name: no-secrets
          pattern: "**/*.env"
          staged: deny
        - name: custom-check
          run: ./scripts/validate.sh

This lets any project using beads define their own preflight checks.

## Implementation Phases

### Phase 1: Static Checklist
- Implement bd preflight with hardcoded checklist for beads
- No execution, just prints what to check
- Update CONTRIBUTING.md to reference it

### Phase 2: Automated Checks
- Implement bd preflight --check
- Run tests, lint, detect stale hashes
- Clear pass/fail/warn output

### Phase 3: Auto-fix
- Implement bd preflight --fix
- Fix vendorHash, version sync
- Integrate with bd-umbf solution for pollution

### Phase 4: Configuration
- .beads/preflight.yaml support
- Make it useful for other projects using beads
- Plugin/hook system for custom checks

## Dependencies

- bd-umbf: Namespace isolation for beads pollution (blocking for full solution)

## Success Metrics

- Fewer CI failures on first PR push
- Reduced "fix nix hash" commits
- Contributors report preflight caught issues before CI","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:01:39.587078-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T18:01:39.587078-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lfak","depends_on_id":"bd-umbf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:01:46.059901-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

-{"id":"bd-lfak.2","title":"Implement --check flag with test runner","description":"## Summary
Implement the `--check` flag to run `go test -short ./...` and report results.

## Implementation Details

### Location
File: `cmd/bd/preflight.go`

### Changes Required

1. **Add check execution logic** in `runPreflight()`:
```go
if check {
    runChecks(cmd)  // New function
    return
}
```

2. **Implement test runner**:
```go
func runTestCheck() CheckResult {
    cmd := exec.Command("go", "test", "-short", "./...")
    output, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
    return CheckResult{
        Name:    "tests",
        Passed:  err == nil,
        Output:  string(output),
        Command: "go test -short ./...",
    }
}
```

3. **Define CheckResult struct**:
```go
type CheckResult struct {
    Name    string
    Passed  bool
    Output  string
    Command string
}
```

4. **Output format**:
```
$ bd preflight --check
✓ Tests pass
  Command: go test -short ./...
  
# On failure:
✗ Tests fail
  Command: go test -short ./...
  Output: [truncated test output]
```

### Testing
- Add tests in `cmd/bd/preflight_test.go`
- Test with passing tests (this repo)
- Test with intentionally failing test (mock or temp file)

### Acceptance Criteria
- [x] `--check` flag runs tests (not just prints message)
- [x] Clear pass/fail output with ✓/✗ symbols
- [x] Shows command being run
- [x] Shows output on failure (truncated if long)
- [x] Exits with non-zero code if tests fail
- [x] Works with `--json` flag for programmatic use","status":"hooked","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/polecats/basalt","created_at":"2025-12-30T18:06:30.419315-08:00","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2025-12-30T22:13:39.341845-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lfak.2","depends_on_id":"bd-lfak","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T18:06:30.419875-08:00","created_by":"mayor"},{"issue_id":"bd-lfak.2","depends_on_id":"bd-lfak.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T18:06:48.582603-08:00","created_by":"mayor"}]}

 {"id":"bd-lfak.3","title":"Implement lint check (golangci-lint)","description":"## Summary
Add lint check using golangci-lint to the preflight --check mode.

## Implementation Details

### Location
File: `cmd/bd/preflight.go`

### Changes Required

1. **Implement lint checker**:
```go
func runLintCheck() CheckResult {
    // First check if golangci-lint is available
    if _, err := exec.LookPath("golangci-lint"); err != nil {
        return CheckResult{
            Name:    "lint",
            Passed:  false,
            Output:  "golangci-lint not found in PATH",
            Command: "golangci-lint run ./...",
            Skipped: true,
        }
    }
    
    cmd := exec.Command("golangci-lint", "run", "./...")
    output, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
    return CheckResult{
        Name:    "lint",
        Passed:  err == nil,
        Output:  string(output),
        Command: "golangci-lint run ./...",
    }
}
```

2. **Add Skipped field to CheckResult** (if not already added by bd-lfak.2):
```go
type CheckResult struct {
    Name    string
    Passed  bool
    Output  string
    Command string
    Skipped bool  // Tool not available
}
```

3. **Output format**:
```
$ bd preflight --check
✓ Lint passes
  Command: golangci-lint run ./...

# When tool missing:
⚠ Lint skipped (golangci-lint not found)
  Install: go install github.com/golangci/golangci-lint/cmd/golangci-lint@latest

# On failure:
✗ Lint fails
  Command: golangci-lint run ./...
  Output: [lint errors]
```

- Depends on bd-lfak.2 for CheckResult struct and runChecks() framework

- Test with golangci-lint not in PATH (mock or temp PATH)
- Test with lint errors (intentional bad code)

### Notes
- This repo has baseline warnings documented in docs/LINTING.md
- golangci-lint respects .golangci.yml config if present

- [x] Gracefully handles missing golangci-lint binary
- [x] Shows clear pass/fail/skip output
- [x] Shows lint errors on failure

 {"id":"bd-lfak.4","title":"Implement nix-hash staleness detection","description":"## Summary
Detect when go.sum has changed but vendorHash in default.nix may be stale.

## Implementation Details

### Location
File: `cmd/bd/preflight.go`

### Detection Logic

**Tier 1 (Simple - implement this):**
Check if go.sum has uncommitted changes (staged or unstaged). If so, warn that vendorHash might need updating.

```go
func runNixHashCheck() CheckResult {
    // Check if go.sum has changes
    cmd := exec.Command("git", "diff", "--name-only", "HEAD", "--", "go.sum")
    output, _ := cmd.Output()
    
    // Also check staged changes
    stagedCmd := exec.Command("git", "diff", "--name-only", "--cached", "--", "go.sum")
    stagedOutput, _ := stagedCmd.Output()
    
    hasChanges := len(output) > 0 || len(stagedOutput) > 0
    
    if hasChanges {
        return CheckResult{
            Name:    "nix-hash",
            Passed:  false,
            Output:  "go.sum has changes - vendorHash in default.nix may need updating",
            Command: "git diff HEAD -- go.sum",
            Warning: true,  // Not a hard failure, just a warning
        }
    }
    
    return CheckResult{
        Name:    "nix-hash",
        Passed:  true,
        Output:  "go.sum unchanged",
        Command: "git diff HEAD -- go.sum",
    }
}
```

**Tier 2 (Future - auto-fix):**
If nix is available, compute the correct vendorHash:
```bash
nix-prefetch-url --unpack "https://..."
# or use nix build with --print-out-paths
```

### Key Files
- `go.sum` - Go module checksums
- `default.nix` - Contains vendorHash on line 12

### Output Format
```
$ bd preflight --check
✓ Nix hash current (go.sum unchanged)

# When go.sum changed:
⚠ Nix hash may be stale
  go.sum has uncommitted changes
  Hint: If you modified dependencies, run nix build to get new vendorHash
  
# When nix available (future):
✗ Nix hash stale
  Expected: sha256-KRR6dXzsSw8OmEHGBEVDBOoIgfoZ2p0541T9ayjGHlI=
  Current:  sha256-ovG0EWQFtifHF5leEQTFvTjGvc+yiAjpAaqaV0OklgE=
  Fix: bd preflight --fix
```

### Add Warning field to CheckResult
```go
type CheckResult struct {
    Name    string
    Passed  bool
    Output  string
    Command string
    Skipped bool
    Warning bool  // Yellow ⚠ instead of red ✗
}
```

### Testing
- Test with clean go.sum (no changes)
- Test with modified go.sum (stage a fake change, then reset)
- Test output format

### Acceptance Criteria
- [x] Detects when go.sum has uncommitted changes
- [x] Shows as warning (⚠) not error (✗)
- [x] Provides helpful hint about vendorHash
- [x] Works with --json flag
- [x] Does not require nix to be installed (just git)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T18:06:32.318167-08:00","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2025-12-30T19:29:29.995126-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lfak.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lfak","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T18:06:32.31998-08:00","created_by":"mayor"},{"issue_id":"bd-lfak.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lfak.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T18:06:48.621655-08:00","created_by":"mayor"},{"issue_id":"bd-lfak.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lfak.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:31:30.9704-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma"}]}
 {"id":"bd-lfak.5","title":"Implement version-sync check (version.go vs default.nix)","description":"## Summary
Check that version in `cmd/bd/version.go` matches version in `default.nix`.

## Current State (as of writing)
- version.go: Version = "0.41.0"
- default.nix: version = "0.37.0"
- **These are already out of sync!** This check would catch this.

## Implementation Details

### Location
File: `cmd/bd/preflight.go`

### Key Files
- `cmd/bd/version.go` line 17: `Version = "0.41.0"`
- `default.nix` line 4: `version = "0.37.0";`

### Implementation

```go
func runVersionSyncCheck() CheckResult {
    // Read version.go
    versionGoContent, err := os.ReadFile("cmd/bd/version.go")
    if err != nil {
        return CheckResult{
            Name:    "version-sync",
            Passed:  false,
            Output:  fmt.Sprintf("Cannot read cmd/bd/version.go: %v", err),
            Skipped: true,
        }
    }
    
    // Extract version from version.go
    // Pattern: Version = "X.Y.Z"
    versionGoRe := regexp.MustCompile(`Version\s*=\s*"([^"]+)"`)
    versionGoMatch := versionGoRe.FindSubmatch(versionGoContent)
    if versionGoMatch == nil {
        return CheckResult{
            Name:    "version-sync",
            Passed:  false,
            Output:  "Cannot parse version from version.go",
            Skipped: true,
        }
    }
    goVersion := string(versionGoMatch[1])
    
    // Read default.nix
    nixContent, err := os.ReadFile("default.nix")
    if err != nil {
        return CheckResult{
            Name:    "version-sync",
            Passed:  true,  // No nix file = no sync needed
            Output:  "default.nix not found (skipping nix version check)",
            Skipped: true,
        }
    }
    
    // Extract version from default.nix
    // Pattern: version = "X.Y.Z";
    nixRe := regexp.MustCompile(`version\s*=\s*"([^"]+)"`)
    nixMatch := nixRe.FindSubmatch(nixContent)
    if nixMatch == nil {
        return CheckResult{
            Name:    "version-sync",
            Passed:  false,
            Output:  "Cannot parse version from default.nix",
            Skipped: true,
        }
    }
    nixVersion := string(nixMatch[1])
    
    if goVersion != nixVersion {
        return CheckResult{
            Name:    "version-sync",
            Passed:  false,
            Output:  fmt.Sprintf("Version mismatch: version.go=%s, default.nix=%s", goVersion, nixVersion),
            Command: "Compare cmd/bd/version.go and default.nix",
        }
    }
    
    return CheckResult{
        Name:    "version-sync",
        Passed:  true,
        Output:  fmt.Sprintf("Versions match: %s", goVersion),
        Command: "Compare cmd/bd/version.go and default.nix",
    }
}
```

### Output Format
```
$ bd preflight --check
✓ Version sync (0.41.0)

# On mismatch:
✗ Version mismatch
  version.go:   0.41.0
  default.nix:  0.37.0
  Fix: Update default.nix to match version.go
```

### Future: Auto-fix
For --fix mode, update default.nix automatically:
```go
// Replace version line in default.nix
newContent := nixRe.ReplaceAll(nixContent, 
    []byte(fmt.Sprintf(`version = "%s"`, goVersion)))
os.WriteFile("default.nix", newContent, 0644)
```

### Testing
- Test with matching versions
- Test with mismatched versions (current state!)
- Test with missing files

### Acceptance Criteria
- [x] Reads version from both files
- [x] Compares and reports mismatch
- [x] Shows both versions clearly on mismatch
- [x] Works when default.nix is missing (skip gracefully)
- [x] Works with --json flag","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T18:06:33.380378-08:00","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2025-12-30T19:30:02.607687-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lfak.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lfak","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T18:06:33.382134-08:00","created_by":"mayor"},{"issue_id":"bd-lfak.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lfak.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T18:06:48.64101-08:00","created_by":"mayor"},{"issue_id":"bd-lfak.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lfak.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:31:30.989707-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma"}]}
